tasks.title: Add llama-cpp-2 dependency and build configuration
tasks.description: FR-4/FR-5: Add the `llama-cpp-2` crate to Cargo.toml. This crate compiles llama.cpp from source via cmake and links it as a static library. Verify the project compiles on Windows with cmake available.
acceptanceCriteria: TDD: Write a minimal Rust test that creates a llama.cpp backend instance (llama-cpp-2::LlamaModel) to verify the FFI linkage works
TDD: Test fails if llama-cpp-2 is not properly linked
TDD: Refactor after test passes — ensure no unnecessary feature flags
Verify `cargo build` succeeds in src-tauri/
Task Logging: .specs/local-llm/tasks-log.json -> task_id | title | summary | is_working | timestamp | notes | decisions_made
Code Review: Simplicity First — only add llama-cpp-2, no other dependencies yet
Update Docs: Note cmake build requirement in README if not already documented

tasks.title: Create LLM state module and Tauri command registrations
tasks.description: FR-4: Create `src-tauri/src/commands/local_llm.rs` as a new module. Add `LlmState` to hold loaded model, model ID, and download state. Register module in `commands/mod.rs` and `main.rs`. Add the new module declaration to `lib.rs`.
acceptanceCriteria: TDD: Write test that LlmState::default() creates empty state (no model loaded, no downloads)
TDD: Test fails if LlmState struct doesn't exist
TDD: Refactor — use parking_lot::Mutex like existing DuckDbState pattern
local_llm module is registered in commands/mod.rs
Placeholder commands are registered in main.rs invoke_handler
Follow existing state management pattern from state.rs (Mutex-wrapped Option)
Task Logging: .specs/local-llm/tasks-log.json -> task_id | title | summary | is_working | timestamp | notes | decisions_made
Code Review: Match existing style — look at how DuckDbState is structured
Update Docs: N/A (internal module, no user-facing change)

tasks.title: Implement detect_system_ram command
tasks.description: FR-1: Create a Tauri command `detect_system_ram()` that returns total physical RAM in bytes. On Windows, use the `windows` crate or `sysinfo` crate to query `GlobalMemoryStatusEx`.
acceptanceCriteria: TDD: Write test that returns a positive u64 value on the current machine
TDD: Write test that the returned value is reasonable (>1GB, <1TB)
TDD: Refactor — use sysinfo crate if it simplifies cross-platform support, or Win32 API directly
Command is registered in main.rs invoke_handler
Returns Result<u64, String> consistent with existing command error patterns
Task Logging: .specs/local-llm/tasks-log.json -> task_id | title | summary | is_working | timestamp | notes | decisions_made
Code Review: Surgical Changes — only add RAM detection, don't refactor other commands
Update Docs: N/A

tasks.title: Implement model catalog and list_available_models command
tasks.description: FR-2: Define a `ModelEntry` struct with id, name, description, sizeBytes, ramRequiredBytes, tier, downloadUrl, filename. Hardcode a catalog of 8 models (Qwen 2.5 Coder 3B, Qwen 2.5 3B, Gemma 4 E2B, Phi-4 Mini, Llama 3.2 3B, Qwen 2.5 Coder 7B, Gemma 4 E4B, Mistral 7B). Return via `list_available_models()` Tauri command.
acceptanceCriteria: TDD: Write test that list_available_models returns exactly 8 models
TDD: Write test that all models have required fields populated (non-empty name, positive size, valid tier)
TDD: Write test that fast-tier models have ramRequired <= 4GB and balanced-tier <= 7GB
TDD: Refactor — extract catalog into a function, keep struct Serializable
ModelEntry struct derives Serialize, Deserialize, Clone
Each model has a stable string ID (e.g., 'qwen25-coder-3b-q4km')
Download URLs point to HuggingFace GGUF files (bartowski or unsloth repos)
Task Logging: .specs/local-llm/tasks-log.json -> task_id | title | summary | is_working | timestamp | notes | decisions_made
Code Review: Simplicity First — hardcoded catalog, no external fetch needed
Update Docs: N/A

tasks.title: Implement download_model with progress events
tasks.description: FR-3: Create `download_model(app, model_id)` Tauri command. Download GGUF from HuggingFace URL to `{app_data_dir}/models/{filename}`. Emit `local-llm:download-progress` events with bytes downloaded, total, and percent. Support cancellation via AtomicBool. Create models directory if it doesn't exist.
acceptanceCriteria: TDD: Write test that models directory is created on download attempt
TDD: Write test that download emits progress events (mock or small file)
TDD: Write test that existing file matching expected size skips download
TDD: Write test that cancel_download sets the cancellation flag
TDD: Refactor — extract download logic into a helper function
Uses reqwest (already in dependencies) for HTTP download
Progress emitted via app.emit() as Tauri events
Partial downloads are cleaned up on error/cancel
Only one download at a time (reject concurrent with error)
Task Logging: .specs/local-llm/tasks-log.json -> task_id | title | summary | is_working | timestamp | notes | decisions_made
Code Review: Use existing reqwest dependency, don't add new HTTP crate
Update Docs: N/A

tasks.title: Implement list_downloaded_models and delete_model commands
tasks.description: FR-3: `list_downloaded_models()` scans the models directory for GGUF files and returns metadata (matching against catalog entries). `delete_model(model_id)` removes the GGUF file from disk and unloads it if loaded.
acceptanceCriteria: TDD: Write test that list_downloaded_models returns empty when no files exist
TDD: Write test that list_downloaded_models returns entry after a file matching a catalog filename is placed in models dir
TDD: Write test that delete_model removes the file and returns Ok
TDD: Write test that delete_model returns error for unknown model_id
TDD: Refactor — share file path resolution logic with download_model
delete_model unloads the model first if it's currently loaded
Task Logging: .specs/local-llm/tasks-log.json -> task_id | title | summary | is_working | timestamp | notes | decisions_made
Code Review: Surgical Changes — only add these two commands
Update Docs: N/A

tasks.title: Implement load_model and unload_model commands
tasks.description: FR-4: `load_model(app, model_id)` resolves the GGUF path, loads it via llama-cpp-2's LlamaModel::load_from_file, and stores the loaded model in LlmState. `unload_model()` drops the model from state. `is_model_loaded()` returns current state.
acceptanceCriteria: TDD: Write test that is_model_loaded returns {loaded: false, modelId: null} initially
TDD: Write test that load_model with a valid GGUF file succeeds and is_model_loaded returns {loaded: true, modelId}
TDD: Write test that load_model with missing file returns error
TDD: Write test that unload_model clears the loaded model
TDD: Write test that loading a second model unloads the first
TDD: Refactor — extract model path resolution into helper
LlmState uses Mutex<Option<(String, LlamaModel)>> pattern
RAM check: estimate required RAM (file_size * 1.3) and warn if exceeds detected RAM (non-blocking warning, not hard block)
Task Logging: .specs/local-llm/tasks-log.json -> task_id | title | summary | is_working | timestamp | notes | decisions_made
Code Review: Simplicity First — one model at a time, no model pooling
Update Docs: N/A

tasks.title: Implement generate_chat with streaming events
tasks.description: FR-5: `generate_chat(app, messages, system_prompt)` formats the messages using llama.cpp's chat template, creates a sampling context (temperature 0.6, top_p 0.95, max_tokens 2048), and streams tokens via `local-llm:token` Tauri events. Emits `local-llm:done` on completion. Supports `stop_generation()` via AtomicBool.
acceptanceCriteria: TDD: Write test that generate_chat returns error when no model is loaded
TDD: Write test that generate_chat emits local-llm:token events when model is loaded (integration test with small model)
TDD: Write test that stop_generation cancels generation and emits local-llm:done
TDD: Refactor — extract message formatting into helper, keep generate loop clean
Uses llama-cpp-2 chat template application (apply_chat_template)
Token loop: llama_decode + llama_sample in a loop, emit each token via app.emit()
Defaults: temperature=0.6, top_p=0.95, top_k=40, n_predict=2048, n_ctx=4096
Thread count defaults to num_cpus but configurable
Generation runs on a background thread to avoid blocking the main thread
Task Logging: .specs/local-llm/tasks-log.json -> task_id | title | summary | is_working | timestamp | notes | decisions_made
Code Review: Goal-Driven — focus on getting tokens flowing, optimize later
Update Docs: N/A

tasks.title: Add inference mode to settings schema
tasks.description: FR-6: Extend the settings flow to support `inferenceMode` ('remote'|'local') and `localModelId` (string|null). These are stored in the existing settings.json alongside llmApiUrl, llmApiKey, llmModel. Default is 'remote' if not set.

TDD: Refactor — no changes to existing settings fields, purely additive
Existing settings.rs remains backward-compatible — old settings files without these keys work fine

tasks.title: Update analyst.svelte.ts to support local inference path
tasks.description: FR-7: Modify `AnalystState.sendMessage()` to branch on `inferenceMode`. When 'local', use `invoke('generate_chat')` + listen for `local-llm:token` events instead of fetch(). When 'remote', keep existing logic. Add `inferenceMode` and `localModelId` to state. Load settings on init(). Handle stop() for local mode via `stopGeneration()`.
acceptanceCriteria: TDD: Write test that sendMessage with inferenceMode='remote' uses fetch (existing behavior)
TDD: Write test that sendMessage with inferenceMode='local' calls invoke('generate_chat')
TDD: Write test that local mode token events append to streamingContent identically to remote SSE
TDD: Write test that stop() calls stopGeneration() in local mode
TDD: Refactor — extract remote and local send logic into private methods to keep sendMessage clean
SQL extraction (extractAndExecuteSql) works identically for both modes
Plan parsing (parseAndSetPlan) works identically for both modes
Messages are preserved when switching modes mid-session
analyst.init() loads inferenceMode and localModelId from settings

tasks.title: Handle model auto-load on app startup and auto-unload after inactivity
tasks.description: FR-4: On app start, if settings have inferenceMode='local' and localModelId is set, auto-load that model. After 5 minutes of no generation activity, auto-unload to free RAM for DuckDB.
acceptanceCriteria: TDD: Write test that app startup with local mode settings triggers load_model for the saved modelId
TDD: Write test that the inactivity timer triggers unload after 5 minutes
TDD: Write test that a generation resets the inactivity timer
TDD: Refactor — use a simple timestamp comparison, no complex timer framework
Auto-load runs in background, doesn't block app startup
If auto-load fails (model deleted), show warning but don't crash
Inactivity timer is only active when a model is loaded
Task Logging: .specs/local-llm/tasks-log.json -> task_id | title | summary | is_working | timestamp | notes | decisions_made
Code Review: Simplicity First — a single timestamp + check, no background threads for the timer
Update Docs: Document the auto-unload behavior in README

TDD: Write test that loading a corrupt/truncated GGUF returns a clear error (not a panic)

tasks.title: Handle rapid mode switching and concurrent operations
tasks.description: Edge cases 8, 10: Switching remote↔local mid-conversation preserves messages. Concurrent downloads are rejected. Generation during mode switch is handled gracefully.
acceptanceCriteria: TDD: Write test that switching mode while streaming stops current generation cleanly
TDD: Write test that concurrent download_model calls return error on the second call
TDD: Write test that messages array survives mode switch intact
TDD: Refactor — use state machine or enum for download/generate state tracking

rules: Write test first (RED) - test must fail
Write minimal code to pass (GREEN) - no optimization
Refactor only after test passes (REFACTOR) - maintain quality
All tasks must follow TDD cycle as acceptance criteria
